title: TIP-1.0 Wire Headers
description: Canonical X-TokenPak-* headers carried on data-plane requests and responses. See https://tokenpak.ai/protocol/transport-bindings for normative text. TIP-1.0 headers are informational metadata; they never alter the request body (Architecture §5.1 byte-fidelity rule).

X-TokenPak-TIP-Version.description: TIP version the emitter is using. Example: TIP-1.0. MUST be present on every TIP-aware request and response.

X-TokenPak-Profile.description: Profile identifier the emitter conforms to. One of tip-proxy, tip-companion, tip-adapter, tip-plugin, tip-dashboard-consumer. See capabilities.schema.json for the authoritative profile list.

X-TokenPak-Capability.description: Comma-separated list of TIP capability labels the emitter publishes for this request. Format: one or more tip.<name> or ext.<ns>.<name> tokens separated by ','. Example: 'tip.compression.v1,tip.byte-preserved-passthrough'.

X-TokenPak-Cache-Origin.description: Classification of any observed cache hit for this response. 'proxy' = TokenPak cache served the response; 'client' = provider cache served it (inferred from response); 'unknown' = cannot attribute (Constitution §5.3 — never over-claim). Required on every response TIP telemetry row.

X-TokenPak-Savings-Tokens.description: Token savings attributed to this request by compression, as a non-negative integer. Cache savings are reported separately; this header never sums compression + cache into one number. Optional on response; absent means 0 or unmeasured.

X-TokenPak-Savings-Cost.description: Cost savings in USD attributed to this request, as a non-negative decimal. See X-TokenPak-Savings-Tokens for cache vs compression attribution rule.

X-TokenPak-Request-Id.description: Stable identifier for this request used for correlating telemetry rows, cache lookups, and log lines. Format: opaque string (UUID v7 preferred). Request-side emitter sets it; response-side emitter echoes it unchanged.

X-TokenPak-Compression-Ms.description: Time spent in the compression stage, in milliseconds. Part of the <50ms overhead budget (Constitution §5 performance principle).
